content.full_text:
y2013

FPPC                                                           Complimentary Copy

Del Norte Municipal League

"The Taxpayers' Watchdog in Del Norte County"

207 PRICE MALL  CRESCENT CITY, CA  95531  PHONE (707) 464-6677

April 1, 1980


Secretary of State
1230 J Street
P. O. Box 1467
Sacramento, CA 95807

                                                             A-80-001-021

Attention: Tina Wolfe, Auditor
           Political Reform Division

Dear Ms. Wolfe:

I am writing on behalf of the Del Norte Municipal League regarding
your letter of February 19, 1980, which was directed to Mr. Richard
Cox, Treasurer. We thank you for forwarding a copy of the "Information Manual and Campaign Disclosure Provisions of the Political
Reform Act" dated January, 1980.  After reviewing this document, we
feel that the Del Norte Municipal League was in error in filing our
Statement of Organization as a recipient committee on February 6, 1980.

The reason for our February 6th application for an identification
number was the understanding (or misunderstanding) that we had received from Del Norte County Clerk John Alexander, to wit: "Any
organization that received contributions of $500 or more in a calendar year and which supported or opposed candidates or ballot measures
was considered to be a recipient committee."  The Del Norte Municipal
League is a taxpayers' association which exists solely upon the dues
and self-assessments by individual, business and organization memberships. The League does not at any time support or oppose candidates
for any public elective office. The League does, however, from time
to time support or oppose ballot measures, governmental administrative activities, and legislation. Our activities are primarily of
an educational nature, which involves informing our members and the
general public about what government is doing; informing government
about the feeling of our members and the general public, and working with the various levels of government to propose efficient ways
to deliver necessary or desired government services.

Following a review of the information manual which you provided
for us, and of the Form 420 and other documents provided, we have
concluded that the League is not presently a "recipient committee",
an "expenditure committee", nor a "major donor committee". The
League has not received $500 or more in contributions in any one
calendar year earmarked for support or opposition of candidates or

                                                             - 1 -

ballot measures, nor have we expended $500 or more for such causes
in any one calendar year. We have thus concluded that we were
under an erroneous assumption that, because: (1) we do occasionally
support and oppose issues and (2) we do receive general fund contri-
butions in excess of $500 in a year, that we thus became qualified
as a "committee" within the meaning of the Political Reform Act
when we were organized on February 1, 1977. However, after discussing
this matter with you by telephone on March 28th, and in subsequent
discussions with Regina Kutchins at the Fair Political Practices
Commission, we realize that we do not presently constitute a "commit-
tee" and have not previously been a "committee".

I explained to Regina Kutchins that we did, from time to time, endorse
or oppose ballot measures, including publicity of these positions in
our regular newsletter, to the media as news releases, and in letters
to others (not mass mailings). I further explained that the Del Norte
Municipal League had not spent an aggregate of over $500 in a year
from our general funds for both things as paid advertising or assist-
ance to some other committee. I also informed her that the League
had not received contributions earmarked for political action in
excess of $500 in a calendar year. She concluded that, based on this
information I had provided to her, the Del Norte Municipal League did
not constitute a "committee" as defined by the Political Reform Act.

Regina Kutchins advised that we submit a letter to you describing the
situation, and asking that our Statement of Organization as a Recipient
Committee be returned or otherwise invalidated, and our I.D. Number be
cancelled. She further advised us that if, at any time in a calendar
year contributions for political purposes or expenditures for politi-
cal purposes reached an aggregate of $500 or more, we would at that
time need to apply for an I.D. Number and comply with the reporting
requirements. We have currently adopted positions on some local
ballot measures and in support of circulating an initiative position
for signatures on one state-wide measure. However, to date we have not
received or expended any monies for any of these. We fully understand
that if we do, and any combination of receipts or expenditures reach
an aggregate of $500, we will need to file for an identification Number
and comply with the necessary reporting provisions of the Political
Reform Act.

Incidentally, I would like to advise you that we have held an annual
officers election this month, and the Treasurer of our organization
is now Mr. Michael Young, who succeeded Richard Cox to this office.

- 2 -

Mr. Young may be contacted through the Del Norte Municipal League
address, or you may communicate with me directly, acting for the
Board of Directors of The Del Norte Municipal League.

Sincerely,

E. W. "Frosty" Godrey, Executive Manager
DEL NORTE MUNICIPAL LEAGUE

cc: Regina Kutchins
Fair Political Practices Commission
110 K. St. Building
Sacramento, CA 95814

Michael Young, Treasurer
Del Norte Municipal League

Gene Schach, President
Del Norte Municipal League

FWG:apw

- 3 -

2 -


888 4th st. 
Phone 1707) 464-6677 
April 1, 1980 
Mr. Young may be contacted through the Del Norte Municipal League 
address, or you may communicate with me directly, acting for the 
Board of Directors of The Del Norte Municipal League.
sections.question_synthetic: Does the Del Norte Municipal League qualify as a 'committee' under the Political Reform Act and does it need to register and comply with reporting requirements?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: Based on informal consultation with FPPC staff (Regina Kutchins), the Del Norte Municipal League does not constitute a committee under the Political Reform Act because it has not received or expended $500 or more in any calendar year specifically for political purposes, despite supporting/opposing ballot measures and issues.
sections.parsing_notes: Format: old; LLM: This is an incoming request/correspondence from an organization seeking to withdraw its committee registration, not a formal FPPC advice letter. The conclusions are based on informal telephone consultation with FPPC staff (Regina Kutchins) rather than a formal written FPPC response. Document references discussions with FPPC but the official response/advice is not included in this document.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Does the Del Norte Municipal League qualify as a 'committee' under the Political Reform Act and does it need to register and comply with reporting requirements?

CONCLUSION: Based on informal consultation with FPPC staff (Regina Kutchins), the Del Norte Municipal League does not constitute a committee under the Political Reform Act because it has not received or expended $500 or more in any calendar year specifically for political purposes, despite supporting/opposing ballot measures and issues.

embedding.summary: The Del Norte Municipal League requests that its Statement of Organization as a Recipient Committee be invalidated and its ID number cancelled, after determining it was mistakenly filed but does not meet the thresholds for committee status under the Political Reform Act.
